edges.description: Orchestrator selects and calls active DesignProvider
Orchestrator starts preview and awaits selection
Orchestrator triggers style detection for existing projects
Orchestrator runs post-processing on downloaded HTML
Orchestrator exports tokens after final selection
IterationEngine updates preview with new variants

external_dependencies.name: Stitch MCP (@_davideast/stitch-mcp)
external_dependencies.purpose: Primary design generation engine via Gemini 3.1 Pro. Provides generate_screen_from_text, generate_variants, extract_design_context, create_design_system, apply_design_system MCP tools.

external_dependencies.name: Node.js http module
external_dependencies.purpose: Local HTTP server for interactive preview. Built-in, zero external dependency.

global_error_strategy.api_error_format: { error: string, code: string, detail?: string }. Codes: PROVIDER_UNAVAILABLE, GENERATION_FAILED, QUOTA_EXHAUSTED, TIMEOUT, POST_PROCESS_FAILED, STYLE_DETECTION_FAILED, TOKEN_EXPORT_FAILED.
global_error_strategy.external_service: Stitch MCP failures trigger automatic degradation to Tier 2 (MOD-003). If Tier 2 also fails, degrade to Tier 3 (text questions in prompt). All failures logged with error code + original error message.
global_error_strategy.logging: Structured JSON logs to stderr. Fields: timestamp, module, level (info/warn/error), message, error_code. No PII or API keys in logs.

auto_decisions.decision: Place new TypeScript modules in src/ui-taste/ directory, parallel to src/lash/
alternatives: Merge into src/lash/ as shared utilities
auto_decisions.rationale: User chose option A. Consistent with Lash architecture: TypeScript provides testable infrastructure, prompt handles orchestration. Separate directory avoids coupling with Lash build-specific code.
auto_decisions.impact: All 9 modules live under src/ui-taste/. CLI exposes subcommands via nopilot CLI.

auto_decisions.decision: Use Promise-based async wait for browser selection callback instead of polling
alternatives: File-based polling (CLI watches /tmp for selection file)
auto_decisions.rationale: Promise + HTTP POST is simplest reliable approach. No file system race conditions. No WebSocket complexity. CLI starts server, returns Promise, Promise resolves on POST.
auto_decisions.impact: Preview server must handle POST /api/select endpoint. CLI blocks on Promise until selection or timeout.

auto_decisions.decision: Store iteration history in-memory (Map<round, DesignVariant[]>) not on disk
auto_decisions.rationale: History is session-scoped and ephemeral. In-memory avoids disk I/O and cleanup concerns. Memory is bounded (max ~10 rounds x 5 variants x ~25KB HTML = ~1.25MB).
auto_decisions.impact: History lost on CLI crash. Acceptable for discover-phase ephemeral data.

auto_decisions.decision: Use regex-based CSS parsing for style detection instead of a full CSS AST parser
auto_decisions.rationale: We need to extract a small set of tokens (colors, fonts, spacing, border-radius) not transform CSS. Regex covers the 80% case for CSS/SCSS/Tailwind config. PostCSS adds a dependency for marginal benefit. Tailwind config is read as JSON-like structure.
auto_decisions.impact: Won't parse complex CSS-in-JS (styled-components, emotion). Accepted risk per REQ-006 ACCEPT_COST.

auto_decisions.decision: Use LLM to convert Stitch designMd to DTCG tokens.json instead of building a deterministic parser
alternatives: Hybrid: regex extract + LLM fill gaps
auto_decisions.rationale: Stitch designMd is free-form Markdown with no stable schema. Deterministic parsing is fragile. LLM extraction handles format variation. Accepted risk per REQ-008 ACCEPT_COST (audit pass rate threshold).
auto_decisions.impact: Token accuracy depends on LLM quality. First-batch human audit at 80% threshold triggers redesign if fails.
